4-3-3.style: Possession-based, high pressing, and expansive wing play (Juego de Posición).

4-3-3 Attacking (4-3-3 Atk).description: Features a single defensive midfielder (DM) shielding the backline, and two highly advanced attacking midfielders (free 8s/Mezzalas) who push into the opponent's box. Full-backs overlap aggressively to provide width.
4-3-3 Attacking (4-3-3 Atk).attacking_structure: 2-3-5 in possession, with both Mezzalas occupying the half-spaces and full-backs/wingers stretching the width.
4-3-3 Attacking (4-3-3 Atk).defensive_structure: Aggressive 4-3-3 high-press. Focuses on immediate Gegenpress upon losing possession.
strengths: Extreme central attacking threat
Overloads the opponent's defensive line
Excellent central-to-wide passing options.
weaknesses: Vulnerable to counter-attacks in spaces behind attacking full-backs
Single pivot can get isolated on transitions.

4-3-3 Defending (4-3-3 Def).description: Uses a double pivot (two defensive midfielders) sitting in front of the back four, with one central/attacking midfielder. Focuses on defensive solidity, mid-to-low block containment, and quick counter-attacks.
4-3-3 Defending (4-3-3 Def).attacking_structure: 4-2-3-1-like transition where the wingers run directly into spaces behind the opposition defensive line.
4-3-3 Defending (4-3-3 Def).defensive_structure: Compact 4-5-1 low-to-mid block, closing down all central corridors and forcing plays wide.
strengths: High defensive stability
Extremely resistant to central penetration
Secure rest defense.
weaknesses: Lack of numbers in opponent box during transitions
Striker can become isolated.

4-3-3 False Nine (4-3-3 F9).description: The central striker drops deep into midfield to draw out opponent center-backs and create a diamond overload in midfield, while the two wingers cut inside diagonally to exploit the central space vacated by the striker.
4-3-3 False Nine (4-3-3 F9).attacking_structure: Transforms into a 3-4-3 or diamond midfield in possession. Wingers act as the main goal scorers.
4-3-3 False Nine (4-3-3 F9).defensive_structure: Compact mid-block pressing, trapping the opponent in central areas where numbers are high.
strengths: Disrupts traditional center-back marking
Creates massive central numerical superiorities
Highly fluid possession.
weaknesses: Requires elite tactical intelligence
Lacks a physical focal point in the box for crosses.

4-3-3.build_up_phase: Center-backs split wide. The Single Pivot drops between or just ahead of center-backs (La Volpe build-up). Full-backs push higher or tuck inside. The goalkeeper acts as the 11th outfield player to create a 3+2 or 4+2 build-up block to bypass opposition's front-line press.
4-3-3.attacking_phase: Creating passing triangles on the flanks (Fullback-Mezzala-Winger). The central striker often drops deep (False Nine) to drag opposing center-backs out of line, opening space for inside runs from inverted wingers. Focus on overloading the wide areas then switching play rapidly.
4-3-3.defensive_phase: Employs high-intensity pressing (Gegenpress) in the opponent's final third. The front three block passing lanes to central pivots, forcing the opponent to play long direct clearances or wide into pressing traps.
strengths: Natural triangles and diamonds facilitate high-tempo possession play.
Creates central numerical superiority (3v2) against flat 2-midfielder shapes (e.g., 4-4-2).
Highly threatening wing overloads and isolations for creative wingers.
weaknesses: Highly vulnerable to counter-attacks in spaces behind advanced overlapping fullbacks.
If the single pivot is aggressively marked or physically overrun, the transition structure collapses.
Susceptible to rapid vertical transitions if the initial counter-press fails.

counters.4-4-2: Overruns the two-midfielder block, creating a 3v2 central advantage and isolating their central defenders.
counters.3-5-2: Exploits space behind their advanced wing-backs using the wide wingers in 1v1 situations.

countered_by.4-2-3-1: The opposing double pivot neutralizes the two attacking No. 8s, while their central attacking midfielder (CAM) targets the space around the single pivot.
countered_by.5-3-2: Deep compact defensive lines with wing-backs block wide spaces, preventing 1v1 wing isolations.

4-2-3-1.style: Modern, balanced transition-focused system with double defensive pivots (Double Pivot).

4-2-3-1 Attacking.description: One deep playmaker paired with an active box-to-box midfielder. Wingers push high, acting as inside forwards, while full-backs overlap aggressively to act as additional wingers.
4-2-3-1 Attacking.attacking_structure: 2-4-4 or 2-3-5 structure, with the CAM, inside forwards, and overlapping full-backs occupying the attacking line.
4-2-3-1 Attacking.defensive_structure: High mid-block pressing, shifting to 4-4-2 with the CAM pressing alongside the striker.

4-2-3-1 Holding (4-2-3-1 Hold).description: Two highly disciplined defensive midfielders sit strictly in front of the center-backs. Wingers stay wider and help full-backs defensively, focusing on stability.
4-2-3-1 Holding (4-2-3-1 Hold).attacking_structure: 4-2-3-1 structure relying on rapid vertical counter-attacks with minimal players committed forward.
4-2-3-1 Holding (4-2-3-1 Hold).defensive_structure: Extremely solid 4-4-1-1 low-block, suffocating space in front of the box.

4-2-3-1.build_up_phase: The double pivot sits in front of the center-backs, forming a secure 4+2 base. If the opponent presses high, one pivot drops deeper or wide to form a 3-man build-up line, making the team highly resistant to pressing.
4-2-3-1.attacking_phase: Rapid vertical transitions. The CAM distributes wide to overlapping full-backs or plays diagonal balls to inside runs by wingers. The single striker holds up play or runs channels to create space.
4-2-3-1.defensive_phase: Transitions into a 4-4-2 or 4-5-1 mid-block. The CAM steps up alongside the striker to lead the first line of defense, while the wide players drop back to protect the flanks.
strengths: Extreme tactical balance; highly secure during defensive transitions due to the double pivot.
Allows the CAM creative freedom in the half-spaces and between the lines.
Excellent spatial coverage of wide zones defensively.
weaknesses: Relies heavily on the single striker's physical ability to hold up the ball and engage center-backs.
If the CAM is marked out of the game, the attacking phase can become stagnant and disjointed.
Vulnerable in midfield if the double pivot splits too wide during build-up.

counters.4-3-3: The double pivot blocks the passing lanes to the Mezzalas, and the CAM exploits the space behind the opponent's single pivot.
counters.3-5-2: Wingers and fullbacks double up on the isolated wing-backs of the 3-5-2.

countered_by.3-4-2-1: The opponent's dual CAMs overload the double pivot, creating a 3v2 situation in central zones.
countered_by.5-4-1: An ultra-dense defensive block completely closes the space between the lines for the CAM.

4-4-2.style: Classic, structured, flat defensive banks, direct attacks and transitions.

4-4-2 Flat.description: Standard flat banks of four. Traditional wingers cross to two central strikers. Flat central midfielders cover central corridors.
4-4-2 Flat.defensive_structure: Highly compact 4-4-2 low-to-mid block, defending narrow.

4-4-2 Diamond (Narrow).description: Matches the 4-1-2-1-2 structure. Midfield diamond controls the center, while fullbacks provide the sole width.
4-4-2 Diamond (Narrow).attacking_structure: Narrow central triangles creating quick one-two passing patterns.
4-4-2 Diamond (Narrow).defensive_structure: Diamond mid-block, trapping the opponent in central zones.

4-4-2 Holding (4-4-2 Hold).description: Features two defensive midfielders instead of flat central midfielders, offering extra cover to the defense, with two wide midfielders playing more conservatively.
4-4-2 Holding (4-4-2 Hold).attacking_structure: Direct long balls to the channels for the two strikers.

4-4-2.build_up_phase: Low-risk, direct build-up. Often plays direct balls to the strikers or feeds the wingers along the touchline to bypass opposition high press.
4-4-2.attacking_phase: Direct wing play. Wingers beat fullbacks on the outside and cross to strikers. Full-backs overlap occasionally but prioritize safety. Strikers work in tandem to create knock-downs and quick combinations.
4-4-2.defensive_phase: Two extremely compact banks of four. The team defends in a narrow low/medium block, denying central passing lanes and forcing the opponent to play wide.
strengths: Solid, predictable, and structurally stable defensive shape that is easy to organize.
Double presence up front keeps opposition defenders under constant threat and prevents them from building up easily.
Highly effective and simple transition channels.
weaknesses: Easily overrun in central midfield by 3-midfielder systems (creating a 3v2 numerical disadvantage).
Spaces between midfield and defense can be exploited by creative CAMs (No. 10s).
Can become too passive and predictable if the opponent has technical midfield superiority.

counters.3-4-3: Exploits the space behind the 3-4-3's two-man midfield by playing quick direct balls to the strikers.
counters.5-4-1: Strikers pin their central defenders, allowing wingers to overload the wide areas.

countered_by.4-3-3: The opponent's central midfield trio easily dominates possession against the two-midfielder block.
countered_by.4-2-3-1: The opposing CAM operates freely between the lines while the double pivot controls the center.

3-5-2.style: Direct attacking, high central presence, wing-back reliant system.

3-5-2 Attacking (3-1-4-2).description: Features a single defensive midfielder (DM) and two attacking No. 8s (Mezzalas) who make advanced runs into the penalty area alongside the strikers. Wing-backs push very high.
3-5-2 Attacking (3-1-4-2).attacking_structure: 3-1-4-2 / 3-1-6, overloading the opponent box with 4 to 6 runners.
3-5-2 Attacking (3-1-4-2).defensive_structure: Aggressive counter-pressing, turning into 5-3-2 when pushed deep.

3-5-2 Defensive (5-3-2).description: Three compact central midfielders prioritize defensive screening. Wing-backs stay deeper and only join the attack on secure transitions.
3-5-2 Defensive (5-3-2).attacking_structure: Direct vertical counters using the striker duo.

3-5-2.build_up_phase: The three center-backs circulate the ball. The wing-backs push high. One of the central midfielders drops to receive the ball, forming a diamond/box passing structure with the backline.
3-5-2.attacking_phase: Direct balls to the striker duo. Wing-backs cross into a heavily populated penalty box consisting of the two strikers and late-running central midfielders. Central midfielders also exploit edge-of-the-box shoot zones.
3-5-2.defensive_phase: Transitions into a 5-3-2 low block. The wing-backs drop deep to form a 5-man defense. The three central midfielders remain compact to deny space in central areas.
strengths: Overloads central areas with 3 center-backs and 3 central midfielders.
Two strikers put maximum physical pressure on standard 2-center-back defensive systems.
Exceptional defensive stability inside the box against crosses.
weaknesses: Flanks are highly exposed if wing-backs are caught high up the pitch during transitions.
Highly vulnerable to rapid, wide counter-attacks (especially against 4-3-3 or 3-4-3 wingers).
Overly dependent on wing-backs' individual quality and fitness.

counters.4-4-2: Outnumbers their central midfield (3v2) and overpowers their two center-backs with two strikers.
counters.4-1-2-1-2: Matches their central diamond while maintaining a three-man defense to handle their two strikers.

countered_by.4-3-3: The opposing wingers exploit the spaces behind the wing-backs, pinning the back-three and isolating the wide center-backs.
countered_by.3-4-3: Matches the backline but overloads the flanks with wingers and wingbacks.

3-4-3.style: High-intensity pressing, aggressive wing-overloads, attacking shape.

3-4-3 Flat.description: Three flat forwards (left/right wingers + striker) and a flat four-man midfield. Emphasizes wide combinations, overlaps, and direct wing dribbling.
3-4-3 Flat.defensive_structure: High pressing 3-4-3 block, turning into 5-4-1 deep block.

3-4-2-1 (Narrow 3-4-3).description: Two narrow attacking midfielders (No. 10s) sit in the half-spaces behind the single striker, while wing-backs provide the width.
3-4-2-1 (Narrow 3-4-3).attacking_structure: Creates central box overloads (pivots + No. 10s), combining short passes through the center.

3-4-3 Diamond (Cruyff Style).description: Features a midfield diamond: one DM (holding), two wide midfielders, and one CAM (playmaker) behind a front three. Focuses on high technical dominance and positional rotation.
3-4-3 Diamond (Cruyff Style).attacking_structure: Fluid 3-4-3 diamond, dominating central and wide zones with constant triangles.
3-4-3 Diamond (Cruyff Style).defensive_structure: Aggressive, high pressing, and high risk defensive line.

3-4-3.build_up_phase: The three center-backs circulate possession with the two central midfielders forming a box, creating passing lanes out of defense.
3-4-3.attacking_phase: Massive overloads in the final third. Five players (two wing-backs + three forwards) flood the opponent's backline.
3-4-3.defensive_phase: Employs an aggressive high press to win the ball immediately. If bypassed, transitions to a 5-4-1 low-block.
strengths: Puts immense pressure on opposition backlines with high overloads.
Excellent for aggressive, front-foot defensive pressing.
Flexible backline transitions.
weaknesses: Vulnerable to long balls in channels behind advanced wing-backs.
Midfield double pivot can get easily overrun if not supported by wide forwards.
Physically exhausting for center-backs and wing-backs.

counters.5-4-1: Stretches their low block defense and breaks it down through wing-back overloads.
counters.3-5-2: Wingers isolate the wide center-backs, breaking down the 3-man backline.

countered_by.4-3-3: Opponent exploits wide channels behind advanced wing-backs rapidly.
countered_by.4-2-3-1: Double pivot holds the center, and the CAM exploits spaces behind the two central midfielders.

possession_tiki_taka.description: Short, rapid passing, high possession, patient build-up, and high defensive line to suffocate opponents in their half (Juego de Posición).
tactical_requirements: High technical capability and passing accuracy of all players including Goalkeeper.
Elite spatial awareness and constant movement to create passing lines.
Intense, immediate counter-pressing (6-second rule) to win the ball back.
effective_against: Low block defensive teams lacking counter-attack speed.
Slow transition systems with low stamina.
weakness: Lightning-fast vertical counter-attacks exploiting the space behind the high line.
Highly disciplined low blocks with physical strikers.

tactical_countermeasures.Facing Gegenpressing: Rotate positions rapidly, drop the pivots deeper to draw out the press, and play quick third-man runs.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing Deep Low Block: Keep maximum width, utilize rapid diagonal switches, and exploit half-spaces with underlapping runs.

direct_counter_attack.description: Sitting deep in a low/medium block to absorb pressure, winning the ball, and launching rapid vertical passes to quick forwards behind the opponent's defensive line.
tactical_requirements: Fast-paced wingers/strikers with elite acceleration.
Deep-lying playmakers with precise long-ball distribution.
Highly disciplined, compact defensive coordination.
effective_against: Aggressive pressing teams that commit too many players forward.
weakness: Highly disciplined low blocks that refuse to leave space behind their defensive line.

tactical_countermeasures.Facing Tiki-Taka: Maintain a highly compact low block, deny central space, and exploit the space behind their high-line fullbacks immediately on transition.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing Wing Play: Double up on wide areas, block crosses, and launch counters through the vacated wide spaces.

gegenpressing.description: Pressing the opponent immediately after losing the ball to force turnovers high up the pitch when they are transitioning and disorganized.
tactical_requirements: Elite physical stamina and speed.
Coordinated team pressing triggers (e.g., bad touch, back-pass).
Highly aggressive, synchronized line movements.
effective_against: Teams trying to play out slowly from the back.
Technical build-up teams that struggle under physical pressure.
weakness: Direct long-ball teams who bypass the midfield press entirely by playing route-one football.

tactical_countermeasures.Facing Deep Low Block: Press high to win the ball near their box, preventing them from starting any transition.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing Long Ball: Ensure the center-backs drop early to win aerial duels, while midfielders sweep second balls.

park_the_bus_low_block.description: Defending deep inside own penalty box, prioritizing structural shape over ball recovery, and leaving no space behind.
tactical_requirements: Tall, physically dominant center-backs for aerial control.
High concentration levels and positional discipline.
Compact horizontal and vertical lines.
weakness: Elite individual dribblers inside the box.

tactical_countermeasures.Facing Tiki-Taka: Block the half-spaces, keep the box congested, and restrict passing lanes to the central striker.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing Wing Play: Deploy wide midfielders to assist fullbacks, ensuring 2v1 coverage on the flanks.

vertical_tiki_taka.description: Combining short, rapid passing combinations with direct vertical progression to break lines as quickly as possible.
tactical_requirements: Creative central pivot players with elite vision.
High-velocity vertical runs from wide players and strikers.
Fluid player rotations in midfield.

tactical_countermeasures.Facing High Press: Use quick wall-passes and third-man combinations to bypass the initial pressing line.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing Low Block: Use quick central combinations to pull defenders out, then exploit the space behind them.

wing_play.description: Overloading the wide areas to create crossing opportunities for physically dominant strikers.
tactical_requirements: Overlapping fullbacks/wingbacks.
Elite crossing capability from wingers.
Strikers with superior heading and physical attributes.
weakness: Five-back defensive systems with wide cover.

tactical_countermeasures.Facing Narrow Blocks: Quickly switch play from one side to another to create 1v1 situations for wingers.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing Counter-attacks: Ensure defensive pivots stay deep to cover the spaces vacated by overlapping fullbacks (Rest Defense).

long_ball_target_man.description: Bypassing the midfield entirely by playing direct long balls to a physically imposing striker who wins aerial duels and knocks the ball down for supporting runs.
tactical_requirements: Target Man striker with elite physical strength.
Aggressive second-ball midfielders.
Accurate long-ball distributors (Goalkeeper or Center-backs).

tactical_countermeasures.Facing High Press: Play direct to the target man immediately, completely neutralizing the opponent's press.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing Low Block: Use the target man to draw defenders, knock down balls for late runners, or secure set-pieces.

fluid_counter_attack.description: Flexible transition style where players swap positions dynamically during counters to pull defenders out of position.
tactical_requirements: Highly versatile attackers.
Elite transition speed and decision making.
Fluid position rotation.
weakness: Teams with quick defensive transition recovery.

tactical_countermeasures.Facing Possession Teams: Allow them possession, wait for pressing triggers in the mid-block, then transition with rapid, rotating runs.
tactical_countermeasures.Facing High Press: Play wide to the wingbacks/fullbacks to stretch the press, then combine centrally.
